td.note {padding: 6pt; border-radius: 4%; } .namedNote, .NoteDisplay {border-radius: 4%; } .singleNote {border-radius: 4%; border-top-left-radius: var(--singleNote-top-left-radius);} table.fretTable {border-spacing: 5pt; } .instrumentBackground {background-color: black; } .nut {border-color: #ab0000; } .noteWhiteKey {background-color: white; color: transparent; box-shadow: 0 0 2pt 1pt pink; } .noteBlackKey {background-color: #555; color: transparent; box-shadow: 0 0 2pt 1pt cornflowerblue; } :root { --nut-gradient-color: #ab0000; --diamonds-color: green; --diamonds-background-color: transparent; --double-diamonds-color: red; --diamonds-size: 1.5em; --note-white-shadow-color: black; --note-black-shadow-color: black; --instrument-margin-tb: 1em; --cell-spacing: 5pt; --named-note-radius: 4%; --border-image-black-key: none; --border-image-white-key: none; --instrument-border-image: none; }

USER 6-string Guitar EADGBE 64,59,55,50,45,40
You can customize the instrument with ID: USER
Any of these fields may be left blank.

Empty, or one or more pairs of id:fret to specify where to block out a nut postion up the net, such as is used for Banjo or using a Capo.
"0" is the first string, and 0 is the first position, which places the nut in the normal position. The nut is fret 0. Thus the first fret is fret 1.
{"zero-based-string-id": zero-based-fret-number}
Follow the braces, commas, and quotes exactly as shown in these examples.
Examples: {"4":5} makes a BanjoNut/Capo at the fifth fret on the fifth string.
{"0":2, "1":4} makes a BanjoNut at the second fret on the first string, and one on the fourth fret of the second string.
{"0":2, "1":2, "2": 2, "3": 2, "4":2} makes a Drop-D style capo at the second fret covering the first five strings.
{} Resets the BN to be a standard nut.

Give your tuning a name.

Desktop

Buttons for Mobile instead of keyboard shortcuts.
